findings.description: The plan standardizes new browser calls on `Authorization: Bearer` and `X-Coordinator-API-Key`, but the current coordinator auth helper only accepts `X-API-Key` via `x_api_key: Header(None)`. The CORS allow-list also omits `X-API-Key`, so existing authenticated browser calls would fail unless the auth helper is explicitly extended.

`POST /agents/{agent_id}/kick` is specified without a `change_id`, but the required registry mutation shells out to `worktree.py teardown <change_id> --agent-id <id> --force`. The registry is keyed by change_id plus agent_id, so an agent-only route cannot reliably identify or remove the right entry, especially if an agent id appears in multiple changes.

Destructive actions are gated only by frontend consent prompts. The `DELETE /locks/{file_path}` and `POST /agents/{agent_id}/kick` contracts accept bare authenticated requests, so scripts or agents with an API key can bypass consent entirely. This violates the codeviz reversibility rule that destructive writes require explicit per-operation consent across API surfaces.

The polling fallback depends on `GET /audit?since=<iso>&change_id=<id>&limit=<n>`, but the current HTTP endpoint only accepts `agent_id`, `operation`, and `limit`; even `AuditService.query` has no `change_id` filter. Without adding these filters or a new scoped audit endpoint, swimlane polling cannot be correctly scoped to a change.

The board requires `work_queue.title`, `claimed_by`, and `claimed_at`, but existing issue responses expose `title` from `work_queue.description`, `assignee`, `created_at`, and `completed_at`; they do not include `claimed_by` or `claimed_at`. The artifacts need either a response-shape extension and tests, or the card requirement should be rewritten to use fields actually returned by `POST /issues/list`.

The MVP relies on one `POST /issues/list` round trip, but `IssueService.list_issues` caps results at 100 and post-filters labels after fetching. This can silently omit active cards or recent completed cards once the coordinator has more than 100 issues. The renderer plan already treated this as a correctness ceiling; the Kanban needs pagination/server-side label filtering or an explicit cap-overrun guard.

The SSE/audit requirements and tasks refer to `AuditService.append`, but the current service exposes `log_operation()` and `_insert_audit_entry()`, not `append()`. The tasks also say to install an audit_log trigger and wire the service to emit through the bus while forbidding a parallel NOTIFY pathway, which is internally ambiguous and risks either duplicate events or an impossible implementation.

Task 2.10 says `GET /worktrees/active` should read the registry via existing `worktree.py list` JSON output, but `worktree.py list` currently prints only a human table and has no JSON flag. Specify direct registry parsing through shared code, add a JSON mode to `worktree.py list`, or reuse `skills.shared.active_agents` for the projection.
